title: posteriordb: Testing, Benchmarking and Developing Bayesian Inference Algorithms
bestFor: Benchmarking Bayesian inference algorithms against curated models, data, and reference posterior draws.
note: Useful when the research purpose is algorithm comparison, posterior accuracy, scalability, or reproducible Bayesian examples.

title: Benchmarking Framework for Performance-Evaluation of Causal Inference Analysis
bestFor: Evaluating average and conditional treatment effect estimators when true counterfactual structure is known.
note: Combines real covariates with simulated treatment assignments and outcomes for causal method validation.

title: Atlantic Causal Inference Conference Data Analysis Challenge 2017
bestFor: Comparing causal estimators under controlled data-generating processes and known estimands.
note: Especially helpful for research on overlap, model misspecification, and estimator robustness.

title: Evaluating the Econometric Evaluations of Training Programs with Experimental Data
dataset: National Supported Work / LaLonde NSW data
bestFor: Teaching and testing matching, weighting, regression adjustment, and sensitivity to observational controls.
note: Classic job-training benchmark used to compare experimental and nonexperimental causal estimates.

title: Bayesian Nonparametric Modeling for Causal Inference
dataset: Infant Health and Development Program semi-synthetic benchmark
bestFor: Evaluating individual treatment effect methods and Bayesian additive regression tree variants.
note: Common benchmark created from randomized trial covariates with simulated potential outcomes.

title: The M4 Competition: 100,000 Time Series and 61 Forecasting Methods
bestFor: Comparing statistical, machine-learning, and hybrid forecasting methods across many frequencies.
note: Large-scale forecasting benchmark with yearly, quarterly, monthly, weekly, daily, and hourly series.

title: The M5 Competition: Background, Organization, and Implementation
bestFor: Research on hierarchical forecasts, sparse demand, intermittent sales, and reconciliation metrics.
note: Daily Walmart product sales with calendar and price features; useful for applied forecasting methodology.

title: National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey
authors: CDC National Center for Health Statistics
bestFor: Survey-weighted regression, population estimation, measurement protocols, and missingness in health studies.
note: Nationally representative health examination survey with weights, strata, clusters, lab files, and questionnaires.

title: The General Social Survey: A National Data Resource for the Social Sciences
bestFor: Analyzing repeated cross-sectional public opinion data with complex questionnaire design and changing modules.
note: Useful for social statistics, item nonresponse, trend estimation, and demographic adjustment examples.

title: Assessing the Risks to Online Polls
dataset: National Public Opinion Reference Survey and online poll datasets
bestFor: Studying online polling bias, weighting adjustment, survey mode effects, and representativeness.
note: Good applied dataset family for comparing probability and nonprobability survey samples.

title: NTIA Internet Use Survey Datasets
bestFor: Applied survey analysis on internet adoption, subgroup estimates, and public-use microdata workflows.
note: Public datasets include documentation and multiple file formats, making them practical for statistics teaching and research.

title: IMAGIC-500: IMputation benchmark on A Generative Imaginary Country
bestFor: Benchmarking imputation under controlled MCAR, MAR, and MNAR mechanisms on structured socioeconomic data.
note: Designed for reproducible missing-data studies where true complete values and missingness mechanisms matter.

title: Imputation of Data Missing Not at Random: Artificial Generation and Benchmark Analysis
bestFor: Comparing imputation methods when missingness depends on unobserved or value-dependent mechanisms.
note: The paper describes multiple MNAR generation strategies and benchmark comparisons across datasets and missingness levels.

title: MIMIC-IV, a Freely Accessible Electronic Health Record Dataset
bestFor: Clinical event-history modeling, longitudinal health trajectories, missingness, and time-to-event prediction.
note: Requires PhysioNet credentialing and data-use training because it contains deidentified clinical records.

title: SEER Cancer Statistics Review and Research Data
bestFor: Survival curves, hazard models, competing risk methods, population cancer statistics, and registry-based studies.
note: Public research data are available after agreement; strong fit for applied biostatistics and epidemiology methods.

title: The National Longitudinal Study of Adolescent to Adult Health
bestFor: Panel data, clustered samples, attrition, adolescent health trajectories, and multilevel survey models.
note: Includes public-use and restricted-use options; access terms differ by file type.

title: The Panel Study of Income Dynamics: Overview, Recent Innovations, and Potential for Life Course Research
bestFor: Repeated-measures models, life-course analysis, family clustering, and household economic trajectories.
note: Long-running panel survey with many modules, weights, and family-linking variables.

title: American Community Survey Public Use Microdata Sample
bestFor: Weighted population estimation, replicate-weight variance estimation, demographic modeling, and small-area examples.
note: Core public-use microdata source for official statistics with detailed survey design documentation.

title: Current Population Survey Annual Social and Economic Supplement
authors: U.S. Census Bureau and Bureau of Labor Statistics
bestFor: Survey-weighted income, poverty, labor, demographic, and policy analysis with repeated annual files.
note: Useful for public statistics workflows, variance estimation, and longitudinal-style repeated cross-section research.

title: The Effects of Small Classes on Academic Achievement: The Results of the Tennessee Class Size Experiment
bestFor: Studying treatment effects in education experiments with clustering, repeated measurements, and classroom assignment.
note: A canonical social-science experiment for teaching clustered designs and finite-sample treatment-effect estimation.

title: The Oregon Health Insurance Experiment: Evidence from the First Year
dataset: Oregon Health Insurance Experiment public-use data
bestFor: Studying noncompliance, lottery-based assignment, intent-to-treat effects, and instrumental-variable estimation.
note: The NBER page provides public-use data and replication code for the Oregon Medicaid lottery study.

title: Cohort Profile: The Health and Retirement Study
bestFor: Longitudinal aging research, repeated measures, attrition, and survey-weighted analysis of older adults.
note: A major longitudinal household survey with public and restricted files; registration is required for downloads.

title: ANES Time Series Cumulative Data File
dataset: ANES Time Series Cumulative Data File
bestFor: Analyzing survey trends, mode changes, subgroup estimation, and repeated cross-sectional political survey data.
note: The cumulative file harmonizes repeated ANES surveys and includes weights for recent mixed-mode samples.

title: Interoperable and Accessible Census and Survey Data from IPUMS
dataset: IPUMS USA census and ACS microdata
bestFor: Population estimation, survey-weighted regression, historical microdata analysis, and public-use sample design.
note: IPUMS harmonizes census and survey microdata, making it useful for methods that need large public-use samples.

title: The World Values Survey: A Source of Comparative Social Data
dataset: World Values Survey longitudinal and wave files
bestFor: Cross-national survey analysis, repeated cross-sectional models, and measurement comparability research.
note: The World Values Survey provides multiple waves and integrated files for comparative survey methodology.

title: A Model to Predict Survival in Patients with Primary Biliary Cirrhosis
dataset: Mayo Clinic Primary Biliary Cirrhosis data
bestFor: Teaching Cox models, censoring, clinical prognostic modeling, and survival-model diagnostics.
note: The dataset is distributed in the R survival package and is a standard example for survival analysis.

title: Treatment of Inoperable Lung Cancer
authors: Veterans Administration Lung Cancer Study Group
dataset: Veterans Administration lung cancer trial data
bestFor: Comparing treatment groups with censored survival outcomes in a classic randomized clinical trial example.
note: A compact survival-analysis benchmark included in the R survival package.

title: External Validation of a Cox Prognostic Model: Principles and Methods
dataset: Rotterdam and GBSG breast cancer survival data
bestFor: Validating prognostic survival models, calibration, discrimination, and external validation workflows.
note: The Rotterdam and GBSG datasets are common survival-model validation examples.

title: A Controlled Trial to Improve Care for Seriously Ill Hospitalized Patients
bestFor: Clinical prediction, survival modeling, missing covariates, and validation of prognostic models.
note: The dataset is widely used in regression modeling and clinical prediction examples.

title: Epidemiological Approaches to Heart Disease: The Framingham Study
bestFor: Cohort analysis, cardiovascular risk prediction, repeated measurements, and time-to-event methods.
note: Access varies by file and is commonly routed through controlled research data systems.

title: The National Longitudinal Surveys of Youth Labor Market Experience
bestFor: Panel-data modeling, life-course trajectories, attrition, and repeated survey measurement.
note: A long-running labor and life-course panel with detailed public documentation.

title: Cohort Profile: The China Health and Retirement Longitudinal Study
dataset: China Health and Retirement Longitudinal Study
bestFor: Aging research, longitudinal health modeling, clustered survey analysis, and cross-wave panel studies.
note: CHARLS is a public longitudinal survey for aging and health research in China.

title: The M3-Competition: Results, Conclusions and Implications
bestFor: Benchmarking forecasting methods and comparing statistical forecasts across multiple time-series frequencies.
note: An influential pre-M4 forecasting benchmark with many series and published competition results.

title: The Tourism Forecasting Competition
bestFor: Forecasting tourism demand, evaluating forecast accuracy, and studying hierarchical tourism series.
note: A domain-specific forecasting benchmark used in applied time-series research.

title: Probabilistic Energy Forecasting: Global Energy Forecasting Competition 2014 and Beyond
bestFor: Evaluating probabilistic forecasts, prediction intervals, and energy load or price forecasting methods.
note: A standard benchmark for probabilistic forecasting with multiple energy forecasting tracks.

title: Estimation in Parallel Randomized Experiments
bestFor: Teaching hierarchical Bayesian modeling, shrinkage, partial pooling, and MCMC pathologies.
note: A small but famous benchmark for Bayesian hierarchical models.

title: Data Analysis Using Regression and Multilevel/Hierarchical Models
bestFor: Teaching multilevel regression, county-level variation, shrinkage, and partial pooling.
note: A widely used applied example for hierarchical regression and multilevel modeling.

title: Randomized Experiments from Non-random Selection in U.S. House Elections
bestFor: Studying regression-discontinuity designs, local treatment effects, and close-election identification.
note: Close-election examples are commonly distributed through regression-discontinuity teaching and software resources.

title: Minimum Wages and Employment: A Case Study of the Fast-Food Industry in New Jersey and Pennsylvania
dataset: New Jersey and Pennsylvania fast-food minimum-wage data
bestFor: Teaching difference-in-differences, policy evaluation, and panel comparisons with before-after treatment timing.
note: A classic applied econometrics dataset for policy evaluation and causal inference.
